full_content:
Owner directive S327 (end-of-session): release path for clean-adopter productization.

The next release opportunity is AFTER GTKB-ISOLATION-017 closes, not now. v0.7.0-rc1 target (not 0.6.2 — too small a label for complete clean-adopter productization).

Sequence (do not skip, do not parallelize before Slice 5):
1. ISOLATION-017 Slice 4 (gt project upgrade + migration/rollback) — NEXT, dependency hinge.
2. Slice 5 (clean-adopter test suite + fixtures) — productization proof.
3. Slice 6 + Slice 7 (docs + examples) — parallel after Slice 5.
4. Slice 8 (release-version gate + closeout) — release-candidate gate.
5. Release hardening: ruff full-repo, pytest scope/parallel, version bump, release notes, wheel/sdist smoke.
6. v0.7.0-rc1 push to GitHub + PyPI.

Feature freeze in effect: no new governance scope work until Slice 8 VERIFIED. The three S327 governance Slice 1s (Backlog DB, Term Primer, Term Disambiguation) sit at idle; their Slices 2+ do not advance until v0.7.0-rc1 ships.

Per owner: "Term primer, backlog DB, and disambiguation were useful governance hardening, but the release path now needs a feature freeze except for ISOLATION-017 and release blockers."
explicit_change_request: Owner explicitly stated the release path at S327 turn-end: target v0.7.0-rc1 after ISOLATION-017 Slice 8 VERIFIED; Slice 4 (gt project upgrade isolation + migration/rollback) is the next implementation target; feature freeze on governance hardening; current release-hardening blockers enumerated (ruff red full-repo, pytest timeout, version 0.6.1, release notes stale).
change_reason: Archive S327 owner release-path directive driving the next-release sequence (ISOLATION-017 Slices 4-8 → v0.7.0-rc1) per .claude/rules/deliberation-protocol.md.
